title: Support Evidence, Authorization, and Troubleshooting Method · Fault-Isolation Clinic
summary: A realistic support evidence, authorization, and troubleshooting method ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in causal support incident record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate user goals, technician authority, system ownership, policy, safety, privacy, and escalation before touching state.
Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement, verify, prevent, and document as an evidence loop rather than a memorized script.
Record expected behavior, observed behavior, user impact, scope, frequency, severity, and a reproducible baseline without blaming the user.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Role Boundary
steps.instruction:
debug case — A vague intermittent ticket triggers an unauthorized familiar fix before impact, baseline, recent change, prediction, rollback, or changed-case proof exists. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Separate user goals, technician authority, system ownership, policy, safety, privacy, and escalation before touching state.

Inspect the Role Boundary trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Role Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate user goals, technician authority, system ownership, policy, safety, privacy, and escalation before touching state. For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Role Boundary, keep Role Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Role Boundary, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Verify Document
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Verify Document: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A vague intermittent ticket triggers an unauthorized familiar fix before impact, baseline, recent change, prediction, rollback, or changed-case proof exists. The debug variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify original and changed cases, check side effects, preserve evidence, state remaining uncertainty, and write an accessible handoff.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Verify Document.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Verify Document, keep Verify Document connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify original and changed cases, check side effects, preserve evidence, state remaining uncertainty, and write an accessible handoff.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Verify Document, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Verify Document precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Verify Document, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A vague intermittent ticket triggers an unauthorized familiar fix before impact, baseline, recent change, prediction, rollback, or changed-case proof exists. The debug variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify original and changed cases, check side effects, preserve evidence, state remaining uncertainty, and write an accessible handoff.

For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Verify Document, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: The absence of the original symptom immediately after repair proves durable resolution.
